pr.title: Match some functions using embedding-based similarity workflow
pr.author: malvarezcastillo

summary: Matched a small final set of functions across fighter common, background flash, menu diagram, and sysdolphin particle code after reviewer-requested removal of fakematches. The PR was originally described as an embedding-based similarity workflow for finding high-value decomp targets, but review narrowed the committed changes to non-fake matches with concrete prototypes and simple implementations.
what_changed: Implemented ftCo_800D7268 as a typed fighter helper taking Fighter* and returning s32; it checks FTKIND_KIRBY and calls ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8(fp->gobj), otherwise returning 0.
Updated ftCo_Attack100.h to replace the unknown ftCo_800D7268 prototype with s32 ftCo_800D7268(Fighter* fp), and added the ftKb_Init include needed for the Kirby special-N call.
Implemented fn_800208B0(u8 arg0) in lbbgflash.c by resetting lbl_80433658 state fields and writing arg0 through a BgFlashData2 view.
Updated lbbgflash.h to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for fn_800208B0 with void fn_800208B0(u8 arg0).
Added mnDiagram2_80243A3C(u8 arg0), a simple switch returning TRUE only for case 11 and FALSE otherwise.
Updated mndiagram2.h include guards from MNDIAGRAM2_H to MELEE_MN_MNDIAGRAM2_H and added the mnDiagram2_80243A3C prototype.
Implemented hsd_8039D1EC(void) in sysdolphin particle code, incrementing hsd_804D78E0, clamping it to at least 256, and returning it as u16.
Updated particle.h to replace the unknown hsd_8039D1EC prototype with u16 hsd_8039D1EC(void).
Earlier attempted matches in gm_17C0/gm_1832 and grcorneria were dropped after review because they were identified as fakematches.
smart_moves: Used already-matched functions as references via embedding/cosine-similarity search, then batched related targets with identical or simple patterns.
Kept the final commit focused on functions that could be represented without fakematching, after reviewer feedback.
Converted UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations into concrete prototypes alongside the matching C implementations, improving callsite type information for future work.
Added a precise include for ftKirby/ftKb_Init.h rather than leaving the Kirby helper call undeclared.
Normalized the mndiagram2.h include guard to the project-style path-based form MELEE_MN_MNDIAGRAM2_H.
decomp_lessons: Embedding-based similarity can quickly surface easy matches, especially when a target is structurally identical to an already-matched function, but candidate matches still need normal review standards.
Do not land fakematches just because a similarity workflow found them; reviewer guidance in this PR was to drop all fakematches, including gm_17C0 changes.
Simple functions are valuable when they retire unknown prototypes and clarify data flow, such as Fighter* arguments, u8 parameters, and void/u16 return types.
When matching functions that touch globals or overlay structures, a local cast like BgFlashData2* over lbl_80433658 can express field layout while preserving the known global symbol.
Header cleanup should accompany source matches so future decomp work can reuse the improved signatures immediately.
naming_conventions: Path-based header guards use a namespaced style such as MELEE_MN_MNDIAGRAM2_H rather than a short local guard like MNDIAGRAM2_H.
Address-bearing comments in headers were preserved, e.g. /* 0D7268 */, /* 0208B0 */, /* 243A3C */, and /* 39D1EC */.
Unknown function placeholders were replaced with concrete return and parameter types when evidence was sufficient.
Function names remained address-based where no semantic name was established, such as fn_800208B0, mnDiagram2_80243A3C, and hsd_8039D1EC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body describes using cosine similarity against already-matched functions to rank unmatched functions.

The workflow was: recommend targets by embedding similarity, use the similar matched function as a reference/template, batch related identical-pattern functions, then verify 100% match before committing.

An example from the PR body says gm_8017E630 had 1.000 similarity to gm_8017EBCC, leading to identification of more array-accessor-pattern functions, although those gm_17C0 changes were later dropped from this PR.
Final landed matches are mostly small control-flow or state-update functions, which are good candidates for similarity/template-based matching.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Yes, I would say let's drop all the fakematches. That goes for all the changes to gm_17C0 as well.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.comment: Done, the not-fake versions are in the other PR anyway 😄

follow_up_queries: Find later PR containing the non-fake gm_17C0 array accessor matches mentioned by malvarezcastillo.
Search for remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS near ftCo_Attack100, lbbgflash, mndiagram2, and particle after PR 2126.
Review whether other headers still use short non-namespaced include guards instead of path-based guards.
Look for additional simple functions with high embedding similarity to hsd_8039D1EC, fn_800208B0, or mnDiagram2_80243A3C.
